# Acorn Calculator

A tiny synthetic Python project for agent-recorder fixture generation. It has calculator helpers, text statistics helpers, and deliberately simple tests.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 14, in <module>
  File "/tmp/agent-recorder-suite/runs/pi-test_diagnose/repo/tests/test_calculator.py", line 9, in test_subtract
    assert subtract(5, 3) == 2
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AssertionError
PASSED tests.test_calculator.test_add
FAILED tests.test_calculator.test_subtract: AssertionError: 
PASSED tests.test_text_stats.test_unique_words_basic
PASSED tests.test_text_stats.test_word_count_basic

I attempted to run the suite with `pytest -q`, but `pytest` is not installed in this environment. I then ran the test functions manually with Python.

Failing test identified:

- `tests/test_calculator.py::test_subtract`

Failure:

```python
assert subtract(5, 3) == 2
```

Likely root cause:

In `src/calculator.py`, `subtract(a, b)` currently returns `a + b` instead of `a - b`, so `subtract(5, 3)` returns `8` rather than `2`.

No files were edited.
